title: Wild Norwegian Macaroni Pasta
description: A citrusy, shaggy midnight-craving dish that blends bright lemon with aromatic herbs, cooked in a fondue pot for a cozy, whole30-friendly experience.
instructions: Fill the pot with 3 liters of water and add 1 tsp of sea salt, bringing it to a gentle boil.
Add the dried macaroni and cook until al dente, about 8 minutes.
In a bowl whisk together lemon zest, lemon juice, 1 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp smoked paprika, 1/2 tsp cayenne pepper, and a pinch of sea salt.
Drain the pasta, reserving 1/2 cup of the cooking water.
Return the pasta to the pot and stir in the lemon vinaigrette, diced tomato, finely diced red onion, minced garlic, 1 tbsp capers, chopped dill, and chopped parsley.
Simmer the mixture for 2 minutes to meld flavors, adding reserved pasta water if the sauce is too thick.
Taste and adjust seasoning with extra salt or lemon juice if needed.
Transfer the pasta to serving dishes and sprinkle additional dill on top.
